description_long: This axe features a robust wooden handle with a natural, slightly curved form, reinforced by two black metal bands for added grip and durability. The head of the axe is forged from dark metal, with a broad, angular blade on one side and a pointed spike on the opposite side, making it suitable for both chopping and piercing tasks. The blade is wide and flat, with a subtle bevel along the cutting edge, and the spike is sharply conical. The handle appears textured for improved grip, and the wood grain is visible, suggesting a sturdy hardwood construction. The mounting of the head is reinforced with a square metal collar, providing stability and strength at the joint. There are some markings or engravings visible on the blade, adding a functional or decorative touch.
description: A sturdy axe with a wooden, slightly curved handle, reinforced by metal bands and a square-collared, dark metal head featuring a wide blade and pointed spike. The blade has visible markings and the handle is textured for grip.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wooden handle with metal bands and the square metal collar, with the blade edge barely visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full profile of the wide, angular blade and the pointed spike, as well as the handle's curvature and grip texture.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the pointed spike and the back of the square collar, with the handle and bands clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the blade's flat face, the spike, and visible engravings or markings on the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved wooden handle with visible grain, reinforced by two black metal bands, textured for grip, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, angular single-edged blade with a flat face and subtle bevel, opposite a sharply pointed spike; square collar at the joint, visible engravings.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and collared mounting with visible square metal collar securing the head to the handle.
